---
title: TØ 7. Working with data
pyodide:
  packages:
    - pandas
    - openpyxl
---

In this note we will explore some common operations when working with datasets. 
We will be using the dataset presented in this [paper](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2352340919302264?utm_source=chatgpt.com) on "the absorption characteristics of extracted phytoplankton pigments".

```{python}
import pandas as pd

pigments = pd.read_csv('../../datasets/curated_abs_dataset.csv')
display(pigments)
```

## Plotting 

One of the best ways to explore a dataset is to plot it, below one column of the dataset is 
plotted

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots()

# Plotting
ax.plot(pigments['wavelength_nm'], 
      pigments['chlorophyll-a'], 
      label='Chlorophyll-a') # <1>

# These are settings to make the plot look nice
ax.set_xlabel('Wavelength [nm]') # <2>
ax.set_ylabel('Adsorption')

ax.legend() # <3> 

# This tell Python to display the plot.
plt.show() # <4>
```
1. Here we plot by picking the x-axis as the wavelength from the dataset and the y-axis as measurements of chlorophyll-a
2. Setting the x-axis name. 
3. Show the label in a little box, helps if we plot multiple curves in the same figure.
4. Show the plot.

### Exercise: Plotting

Use the cell below to plot some of the other columns of the dataset.

Try to plot several columns at the same time.

```{pyodide}
#| exercise: plotting
#| setup: true

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

url = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/au-mbg/imol/refs/heads/main/course_notes/datasets/curated_abs_dataset.csv'

pigments = pd.read_csv(url)
```

```{pyodide}
#| exercise: plotting
fig, ax = plt.subplots()

# Your code for plotting here

plt.show()
```

```{pyodide}
#| exercise: plotting
#| check: true

def provide_feedback(envir_result):

  ax = envir_result.get('ax')
  if ax is None:
    return {"message": "You may have deleted the code that creates the figure and axis variables, reset the cell or add the code back in.", "correct": False}
  elif not isinstance(ax, plt.Axes):
    return {"message": "You have changed the <code>ax</code> variable to an incorrect type", "correct": False}

  issues = []

  if len(ax.lines) == 0:
      issues.append("Add the data using <code>ax.plot(...)</code>.")

  if not ax.get_xlabel().strip():
      issues.append("Add a label to the x-axis using <code>ax.set_xlabel(...)</code>.")

  if not ax.get_ylabel().strip():
      issues.append("Add a label to the y-axis using <code>ax.set_ylabel(...)</code>.")

  if issues:
      feedback = {
          "correct": False,
          "type": "info",
          "message": "<p>Your plot is not complete yet:</p><ul>"
                    + "".join(f"<li>{issue}</li>" for issue in issues)
                    + "</ul>"
      }
  else:
      feedback = {
          "correct": True,
          "type": "success",
          "message": "Congratulations you made a plot! Your code passes the checks and appears to produce a complete plot. Now inspect the plot carefully and confirm that the data, labels, and overall appearance match what you intended."
      }

  return feedback


feedback = provide_feedback(envir_result)
feedback

```

### Exercise: Interpreting plots

Chlorophyll a is the main photosynthetic pigment, while chlorophyll b is an accessory pigment found in green algae and land plants. Use the plotted spectra to explain why having both pigments could be useful.
